failure_signature.details: Could not resolve hostname 'api.openai.com': Name or service not known. DNS resolution failed.

problem.goal_state: LangChain agent connecting to OpenAI API in containerized environment

solutions.solution_summary: Fix DNS configuration in the container or host environment
solution_steps: Verify DNS resolution from the host: nslookup api.openai.com or dig api.openai.com
In Docker, ensure the container uses a valid DNS server: add dns: ['8.8.8.8', '8.8.4.4'] to docker-compose.yml
Check /etc/resolv.conf inside the container for valid nameserver entries
If using Kubernetes, verify CoreDNS is running: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l k8s-app=kube-dns
As a temporary workaround, add the IP directly to /etc/hosts: 104.18.6.192 api.openai.com (verify current IP first)

failure_signature.details: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1007). Connection to api.anthropic.com:443 failed.

problem.goal_state: CrewAI agents communicating with Anthropic API behind corporate proxy

solutions.solution_summary: Install corporate CA certificates and configure Python SSL context
solution_steps: Install the corporate CA certificate bundle: pip install pip-system-certs or pip install certifi
Set the SSL_CERT_FILE environment variable to point to the corporate CA bundle: export SSL_CERT_FILE=/etc/ssl/certs/corporate-ca-bundle.pem
Alternatively, set REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE for the requests library: export REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE=/etc/ssl/certs/corporate-ca-bundle.pem
Run the Python certificate installer on macOS: /Applications/Python\ 3.11/Install\ Certificates.command
As a last resort (NOT recommended for production), disable SSL verification: httpx.AsyncClient(verify=False) and suppress warnings

failure_signature.details: Connection refused: [Errno 111] Connect call failed ('127.0.0.1', 6333). Could not connect to Qdrant vector database.

problem.goal_state: AutoGen RAG agent querying Qdrant vector store for knowledge retrieval

solutions.solution_summary: Start the Qdrant service and verify network connectivity
solution_steps: Check if Qdrant is running: docker ps | grep qdrant or systemctl status qdrant
Start Qdrant if not running: docker run -d -p 6333:6333 qdrant/qdrant:latest
Verify the service is accessible: curl http://localhost:6333/healthz should return a 200 response
If running in Docker Compose, ensure the agent container and Qdrant are on the same network and use the service name (qdrant:6333) instead of localhost
Check for port conflicts: lsof -i :6333 to see if another service is using the port

failure_signature.details: ProxyError: Cannot connect to proxy http://corporate-proxy.internal:8080. Connection to proxy timed out after 10.0 seconds.

problem.goal_state: OpenAI Agents SDK running in corporate network with mandatory proxy

solutions.solution_summary: Configure proxy settings correctly for the HTTP client and verify proxy accessibility
solution_steps: Set proxy environment variables: export HTTP_PROXY=http://corporate-proxy.internal:8080 and export HTTPS_PROXY=http://corporate-proxy.internal:8080
Verify the proxy is reachable from the host: curl -x http://corporate-proxy.internal:8080 https://api.openai.com/v1/models
If the proxy requires authentication, include credentials: export HTTPS_PROXY=http://user:password@corporate-proxy.internal:8080
Configure httpx client directly: httpx.AsyncClient(proxy='http://corporate-proxy.internal:8080')
Add NO_PROXY for any internal services that should bypass the proxy: export NO_PROXY=localhost,127.0.0.1,.internal
